title: Passive Skill 14 (Silent Failure Watcher) + ADR-006 (retention architecture)
current_state: Phase 8.7 introduces two new architectural systems (observability + retention) and several new categories of swallowed-failure tracking. The passive skill suite currently has 13 skills covering health checks, drift, debt â€” none surface silent-failure trends. ADRs are checked-in design documents; the retention architecture is novel and warrants its own ADR.
structural_problem: Without a passive-scan window into silent failures, the new observability system records data nobody reads. Without an ADR, the retention design decisions decay into tribal knowledge.
target_state: (a) scripts/passive/silent_failure_watcher.py: 14th passive skill. Reads from data/silent_failures.db; reports top 5 categories by 24h count + WARN if any single category exceeds 50 events / 24h + FAIL if total exceeds 500. (b) /passive command + passive_daily_digest.py wired to include this skill in the scorecard. (c) docs/adr/006-retention-architecture.md captures the policy design + max_age justifications + concurrency model + the choice of file-lock over OS scheduler exclusivity.
files_affected: PI.md (refresh after â€” refresh_pi.py picks up the new skill)
audit_refs: Caps off Phase 8.7 â€” gives observability a feedback loop and retention an authoritative design doc
design_notes: Skill output (matches other passive scripts): write report to reports/silent_failure_watcher.md with Status/Generated/Summary/Sections
Section 1: Top 5 categories by count (PASS/WARN/FAIL per the env thresholds)
Section 2: Recent unique exception types in the top category (to give first-look context)
Section 3: 24h vs 7d delta (is the failure rate climbing?) â€” requires storing daily totals; deferred if too complex for first iteration
Integration with passive_daily_digest: add a row to the scorecard table; same exit code convention 0/1/2

ADR-006 structure: Context (5 unbounded sources) â†’ Decision (Policy dataclass + 4 kinds + DEFAULT_POLICIES) â†’ Consequences (positive: single point of policy; negative: must keep ADR in sync if policies change) â†’ Rejected alternatives (per-file ad-hoc rotation, external logrotate dependency, in-database TTL â€” and why each was rejected)
success_criteria: scripts/passive/silent_failure_watcher.py exits 0/1/2 by thresholds and writes reports/silent_failure_watcher.md
passive_daily_digest.py includes the new skill in its scorecard (14 rows instead of 13)
docs/adr/006-retention-architecture.md exists with all 4 sections (Context / Decision / Consequences / Rejected alternatives)
PI.md Â§6 (passive skills inventory) refreshed by scripts/refresh_pi.py to list 14 skills
risk_notes: Threshold tuning may surface false WARN/FAIL on first deployment as catches that were previously silent are now visible. Mitigation: tune up over the first 7 days; expect a noisy initial scorecard.
migration_plan: 1. Write scripts/passive/silent_failure_watcher.py modeled on existing passive scripts (e.g. memory_pollution_detector.py)
2. Wire it into passive_daily_digest.py (register the new skill at the existing dispatch site)
3. Write docs/adr/006-retention-architecture.md (4 sections as in design_notes)
4. Run scripts/refresh_pi.py to pull the new skill into PI.md auto-sections
5. Add testing/test_silent_failure_watcher.py: seed silent_failures.db with synthetic rows; run script; assert thresholds honored
7. After this ticket all 12 Phase 8.7 tickets are closed; tag the milestone in vault/notes/sprints/ as Phase 8.7 complete
test_plan: test_pass_when_under_thresholds â€” seed 10 rows across 3 categories; assert exit 0; report PASS
test_warn_when_category_exceeds â€” seed 100 rows in one category; assert exit 1; report WARN with that category named
test_fail_when_total_exceeds â€” seed 600 rows total; assert exit 2; report FAIL
test_thresholds_env_override â€” set PI_SILENT_FAILURE_WARN_PER_CAT=5; seed 10 in one category; assert WARN triggered
test_report_file_written â€” assert reports/silent_failure_watcher.md exists after run with correct sections
test_daily_digest_includes_skill_14 â€” run passive_daily_digest; assert scorecard has 14 entries
